behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Integration. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit is possible for a pure payload-builder, but this behavior is about what is actually sent on dispatch, which is a module handoff (classifier -> API client). System rejected because no real network/OS/subprocess is required; a stub transport can expose missing labels. Agentic/Workflow rejected because correctness does not depend on model judgment or multi-step agent chaining. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier + request-construction + API-client adapter with a fake transport; invoke classify() and assert captured outbound payload contains the full taxonomy list every time.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Integration. PASS2-AUDIT: Failure appears at response-handoff boundary (API response parsing/normalization -> public return value), so a single-function Unit test is not the safest minimum. System rejected because real HTTP is unnecessary; a stubbed API response reproduces the failure. Agentic rejected by behavior text: it checks fallback handling for an out-of-taxonomy label, not model quality. Workflow rejected: single decision path. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run classifier with stubbed API client returning an invalid label string and assert module output is exactly "unknown" (and never raw invalid label).

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Unit. PASS2-AUDIT: Behavior is explicitly a local normalization determinism property on identical raw inputs; no module wiring, real infra, or model variability is required. Integration rejected by behavior text: "identical raw API responses" and "normalization step" isolate a pure transformation. Higher levels are disqualified for the same reason. FINAL: Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function/object repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Agentic. PASS2-AUDIT: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live API calls and checks consistency of returned labels, which depends on real model outputs/non-determinism. Integration rejected because mocks/stubs would hide real drift/variance. System considered but rejected: while real HTTP is involved, the core risk is model decision consistency, which is the Agentic boundary. Workflow rejected by behavior text: this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-step agent pipeline. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute repeated real classification calls for same input/taxonomy; record provider raw outputs and final labels; assert label stability when provider response content is unchanged, and allow change only when provider response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Integration. PASS2-AUDIT: The critical failure is enforcement at the pre-dispatch handoff (budget logic -> dispatch decision), which needs module wiring to verify "before dispatch" behavior. Unit rejected because it can miss that dispatch still occurs despite local budget checks. System rejected: no real network/OS needed; fake client can assert dispatch/no-dispatch and payload truncation. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no dependence on model content decisions. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Compose classifier with real budget-enforcement code and fake API client; test over-budget inputs and assert configured truncate/reject policy is applied and dispatch behavior matches policy.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: System. PASS2-AUDIT: This behavior depends on real provider-reported usage accounting from a live API call; mocks can falsely pass and hide provider-side tokenization differences. Integration rejected by behavior text "Provider-reported token usage" because in-process fakes cannot validate real provider accounting. Agentic considered, then rejected: behavior does not evaluate model decision quality/classification correctness; it validates usage telemetry against budget. Workflow rejected: single call check, no multi-step chain. FINAL: System.
behaviors.test_description: Make real API calls for representative documents and assert provider usage metrics (input/output/total tokens per response schema) are <= configured budget for the call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Integration. PASS2-AUDIT: Real failure boundary is retry orchestration across modules (API adapter error -> backoff/wait policy -> retry -> caller result). Unit may miss handoff mistakes; Integration is minimum. System rejected because real network timing is not required to catch logic failures; deterministic stubbed 429 + controllable clock reproduces behavior. Agentic rejected: no model-output-quality dependence. Workflow rejected: retry loop is control-flow, not multi-agentic-step pipeline. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub API client to return rate-limit error then success; run classifier with test clock/backoff scheduler and assert it waits, retries expected times, and returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
